description_long: This object is a delicate flower with elongated, slightly curved petals radiating outward from a small, rounded yellow center. The petals are pale, almost white, with subtle hints of yellow, and are arranged in a radial pattern, giving the flower a star-like appearance. The flower is supported by a slender, slightly bent green stem, which also bears two large, dark green leaves with visible veins and a somewhat wavy, irregular margin. The leaves are ovate in shape and appear to be attached alternately along the stem. The overall structure is lightweight and airy, with the flower head positioned at the top of the stem. The coloration and form suggest a wildflower or ornamental species commonly found in urban parks or gardens.
description: A pale yellow, star-shaped flower with long, slender petals and two large, dark green ovate leaves on a thin stem. The flower has a delicate, airy appearance and a small yellow center.
description_view_0: Side view showing the flower's profile, with petals slightly cupped upward and two large leaves visible along the stem.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the radial arrangement of the petals and the central yellow disk, with both leaves clearly visible.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the spread of the petals and the curvature of the stem, with both leaves seen from a different perspective.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the underside of the petals and the back of the leaves, emphasizing the flower's structure.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration around the central disk, with each petal spaced evenly and slightly overlapping at the base, creating a symmetrical star-like pattern.
